mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >65% of rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >55% of triggered months, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with a t-stat > 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and impending recession, causing equity markets to price in earnings downgrades over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in late-cycle regimes produce positive 20-day forward returns in >58% of instances, or regime-conditional alpha is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher consumer price index readings erode real purchasing power and prompt expectations of tighter monetary policy, generating immediate negative pressure on S&P 500 prices over the next week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases correlate with positive 5-day forward returns in >55% of observations, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5% annualized indicates severe demand contraction, forcing equity valuations to adjust downward over the subsequent month as earnings forecasts are revised.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >57% of low-GDP-growth quarters, or the threshold effect shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, triggering short-covering and positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration in recovery regimes fails to produce positive 5-day forward returns in >50% of cases, or the signal decays to zero IC within 3 trading days.
